channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17906] conf-sanity test_153a: check mount failed
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/aa7ea686-d8e3-4e95-8ea8-f43165f62b3b

test_153a failed with the following error:

check_mount failed

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4518 - 5.14.0-284.30.1.el9_2.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4518 - 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

This test was added in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/54022 "LU-17379 mgc: try MGS nodes faster" and has been failing fairly regularly since it landed on 2024-04-08 - 114 failures in 988 runs.

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
conf-sanity test_153a - check_mount failed

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Merged for 2.17

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57234/
Subject: LU-17906 ptlrpc: reduce time for connection switch
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 2d3036857edade4b73ccaaf20d5eb8cfbc91da93

"Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57234
Subject: LU-17906 ptlrpc: reduce time for connection switch
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: b4f50e2d3d06101d68e9904bc62367eedc00503d

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56755/
Subject: LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: ee977b9039118bbd6a38acd978d7d74c2b2b704f
Lustre 2.16.0 RC5: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/d3e06116-0441-4f88-8111-c99cfbdb76c3
uploaded patch contains both fixes from https://review.whamcloud.com/56699 and https://review.whamcloud.com/56664

"Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56755
Subject: LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 75ddb35f6abe381de2217017b51557886b24bb12

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56736/
Subject: LU-17906 pltrpc: Revert "LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect"
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 8439d360c0e5d145e2e41568ead61e177392a496

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56736
Subject: LU-17906 pltrpc: Revert "LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect"
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 96bcc9c3306def316999d907eecbba4849821632

"Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56699
Subject: LU-17906 lnet: change LNetPeerDiscovered to use LNET_LOCK_EX
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: fe815cc6434b6beabc4576dd47f62cb749846895
Seems like another patch necessary

"Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56664
Subject: LU-17906 ptlrpc: fix bug in import_select_connection()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4fa722bbcec68d75ae3b7c02a23f6fc5db30e9ef
Merged for 2.16

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54286/
Subject: LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 6fe522d3d4f92aa2a48a573419f4590b10ef13d3
+1 on master branch: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/f855f4cf-542f-4736-abed-eb138b4255bb

I've just refreshed pending patch https://review.whamcloud.com/54286

it fits well in fixing such issues and avoiding RPC stuck on peer being discovered
yes, I was thinking about that with similar thoughts, preparing patch to try

I think it should be enough to check their lnet status and don't do mount attempt until it is 'discovered'

That would help the test case to pass, but doesn't really help users trying to mount the filesystem in real life. Should the retry interval be shorter until some real LNet interface is discovered and connected? Before that point, it isn't worthwhile to try and send an RPC, but looping locally on LNet interface discovery doesn't put any load on the server (unlike resending MGS_CONNECT RPCs in a loop from thousands of clients would).

the reason of failure is that all peers are not ready at the mount attempt moment:

00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057645:0:4790:0:(api-ni.c:1591:lnet_nid4_cpt_hash()) Match nid 192.168.252.112@tcp to cpt 0
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057647:0:4790:0:(peer.c:1527:LNetPeerDiscovered()) Peer NID 192.168.252.112@tcp discovered: 0
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057648:0:4790:0:(api-ni.c:1591:lnet_nid4_cpt_hash()) Match nid 10.252.252.113@tcp to cpt 1
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057648:0:4790:0:(peer.c:1527:LNetPeerDiscovered()) Peer NID 10.252.252.113@tcp discovered: 0
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057649:0:4790:0:(api-ni.c:1591:lnet_nid4_cpt_hash()) Match nid 10.240.45.9@tcp to cpt 0
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057650:0:4790:0:(peer.c:1527:LNetPeerDiscovered()) Peer NID 10.240.45.9@tcp discovered: 0
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057651:0:4790:0:(api-ni.c:1591:lnet_nid4_cpt_hash()) Match nid 10.240.45.13@tcp to cpt 0
00000400:00000200:1.0:1725424549.057652:0:4790:0:(peer.c:1527:LNetPeerDiscovered()) Peer NID 10.240.45.13@tcp discovered: 0

so at first cycle of NID trying they all were checked and all were non-discovered. Then import_select_connection() just try them one by one and stuck on first as it is fake one.

Real nodes 10.240.45.9@tcp and 10.240.45.13@tcp were unavailable and that is why test failed. I think that is due to 'reformat_and_config' as first test command, so these nodes are in the middle of discovery process on client. Basically test is not reliable until valid node are discovered. So I think it should be enough to check their lnet status and don't do mount attempt until it is 'discovered'
Failed 13x in the past week in autotest with "check_mount_failed".

I think the Janitor test failures are caused by a 7200s test session timeout that has been exceeded due to new tests being added, which needs to be fixed in the test framework, or the subtests moved to another session.

"Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56235
Subject: LU-17906 tests: debug conf-sanity/151b+153a timeout
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 9b77526ed3548fbcc74a766a835013b4b458932f

This is also failing regularly in Janitor testing, causing it to always report a conf-sanity timeout, and not testing the rest of the subtests. For example:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/gerrit-janitor/45071/testresults/conf-sanity3-ldiskfs-DNE-centos7_x86_64-centos7_x86_64/

Preferably the subtest should be fixed so that it runs properly for Janitor testing, since it is causing all later subtests to be missed. It might be that the choice of "fake" IP addresses is causing problems for this test? There was a problem like this fixed in https://review.whamcloud.com/55890 "LU-17331 tests: fix conf-sanity/30b fake NID generation" because the "fake" NIDs were occasionally conflicting with real NIDs and causing the test to fail.

If there is something specific that makes fixing this subtest difficult, at a minimum some extra debugging should be added to the subtest (e.g. print NIDs, try pinging "fake" NIDs, add some "echo" or "log" lines to show where it is hanging) to be able to understand the issue further.

This subtest is still failing on master, though only 4x in the past week. Both "check_mount failed" as well as "mds1: import is not in FULL state after 50":
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?client_branch_type_id=24a6947e-04a9-11e1-bb5f-52540025f9af&server_branch_type_id=24a6947e-04a9-11e1-bb5f-52540025f9af&horizon=2332800&status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=7f66aa20-3db2-11e0-80c0-52540025f9af&sub_test_script_id=734c548d-b4d1-4057-917e-530bca364e7d&source=sub_tests#redirect

as I see test failed because OST0000 had long mount:

[28778.637062] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: umount -d -f /mnt/lustre-ost1
[28782.689268] Lustre: lustre-OST0000: Not available for connect from 10.240.26.247@tcp (stopping)
[28782.690762] Lustre: Skipped 4 previous similar messages
[28793.344223] Lustre: lustre-OST0000 is waiting for obd_unlinked_exports more than 8 seconds. The obd refcount = 3. Is it stuck?
[28793.417030] Lustre: server umount lustre-OST0000 complete

so client mount happened too early it seems when valid nodes are still having 'non-uptodate' status and that causes NID choosing cycle to finish quickly at first round without finding any valid NID and starts from the first one as result:

           "192.168.252.112@tcp": { connects: 1, replied: 0, uptodate: false, sec_ago: 30 }
          "10.252.252.113@tcp": { connects: 0, replied: 0, uptodate: false, sec_ago: never }
          "10.240.26.241@tcp": { connects: 0, replied: 0, uptodate: false, sec_ago: never }
          "10.240.26.247@tcp": { connects: 0, replied: 0, uptodate: false, sec_ago: never }

Here test expects to find last two NIDs with "uptodate: true" but they are not and test fails

Solution could be just waiting for mgs and ost1 are mounted fully prior mounting client, maybe by checking their lnet discovery status
Mike, it looks like the test that was added in your patch is failing about 1/8 test runs.
